evidence.scientific_clarity: All four panels (pipeline diagram, ablation bars, training curves, 5×5 heatmap) clearly convey the brief's claims; panel labels A–D are bold sans-serif; readable at publication size.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype is NOT set to 42; PDF embeds fonts as Type3 (bitmap-path) with DejaVu Sans — the matplotlib default — rather than editable TrueType/Type42 outlines; svg.fonttype also absent.
evidence.palette: Blue/orange/gray are colorblind-safe (IBM-style); sequential 'Blues' cmap for heatmap is appropriate; green used only for output box (directional OK), not for up/down signal, so no red/green misuse.
evidence.layout_density: GridSpec 2×3 with width_ratios=[2,1,1] matches brief exactly; bottom panel spans full row; hspace/wspace tuned; no gaping whitespace or label overlaps observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches all four panels; legend in panel C is inside the axes (upper-right, no overlap); minor issue: panel A 'Memory'/'Skills' labels are small (6.5 pt) and may be hard to read in print.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is real PDF vector but fonts are embedded as Type3 (Subtype /Type3) rather than selectable TrueType (fonttype=42 not set), so text is not selectable/editable in Illustrator.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (48 KB vector), figure.png (107 KB), figure.svg (106 KB), caption.tex (696 B), and gen_figure.py (8.7 KB) all exist and are non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all panels, describes the pipeline stages, references specific accuracy value (64.7%), mentions faster convergence for panel C, and explains heatmap encoding — specific and complete.

overall_strengths: Well-structured 4-panel hero layout exactly matching the brief's gridspec specification, with clear scientific narrative across all panels.
Caption is quantitatively specific (64.7% accuracy cited) and aligns tightly with every panel; colorblind-safe palette with semantically appropriate color choices.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype=0: fonts are embedded as Type3 (non-selectable paths) instead of editable TrueType outlines, making the PDF unusable for journal production editing.
Default DejaVu Sans font family used throughout; no explicit fonttype settings in rcParams, which is a common reviewer/production red flag for vector figures.
